source_gate.reason: Publication timing is documented, but the NY Fed API/docs checked in this scout did not provide machine-readable point-in-time vintages, release timestamps per observation, or an explicit no-revision/revision-history policy adequate for historical trading research.

documents_checked.finding: Page states Primary Dealer time-series data are updated on Thursdays at approximately 4:15 p.m. with previous week's statistics; data from January 28, 1998 to present are available and split into periods due to data-structure changes.

API documentation exposes list/timeseries, list/asof, list/seriesbreaks, get/asof/{date}, get/{timeseries}, and all-surveys endpoints, but no vintage-as-of or revision-history endpoint was identified.
Series breaks returned: SBP2001, SBP2013, SBN2013, SBN2015, SBN2022, SBN2024, confirming structural-break handling is necessary.
As-of dates are available with series-break labels, but release timestamps and historical vintages are not included.

data_provenance.market_data_block_reason: Source-vintage gate failed before performance evaluation; configured market-data access was not used for a successful OHLCV request.

orthogonality.deferred_reason: Performance/controls intentionally not run because source-vintage safety failed first.

warnings: Do not trade from current NY Fed historical dealer-statistics API output without point-in-time/vintage treatment.
No direct children proposed because this is source-gated/hold.
Primary Dealer Statistics are voluntary and NY Fed page notes the Bank does not audit dealer-reported data.
